Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vladie Viltman, Esq. 415-593-7333 of the Law Office of Brian E. Soriano for Plaintiff.
Rosaline Ayoub, Esq. 213-486-0048 of QPWB for Defendant APRIL PATTERSON, DR PATTY'S DENTAL BOUTIQUE.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT APRIL PATTERSON, DR PATTY'S DENTAL BOUTIQUE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendants April Patterson and Dr. Patty's Dental Boutique's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff Richard Teed fails to show that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts to warrant the imposition of specific jurisdiction over them in California. The declaration of Mr. Teed shows that Dr. Patterson came to California in 2014, generally described her practice, and explained that she could improve Mr. Teed's look. Over the next two years, Mr. Teed and his then girlfriend communicated with Dr. Patterson. The precise content of those communications is unknown. In late 2016, Mr. Teed and his then girlfriend scheduled appointments with Dr. Patterson in Florida, where the dental procedure that is the basis for Mr. Teed's claims occurred. Per Prince v. Urban (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1056 and the authorities cited therein, the foregoing is insufficient for California jurisdiction. "[T]here is a great deal of authority supporting the conclusion that solicitation alone is not sufficient to constitute purposeful availment." (Ghanem v. Kay (D DC 1984) 624 F.Supp. 23, 24.) Mr. Teed fails to present evidence that defendants aggressively solicited him such that the exercise of jurisdiction might lie. Prince notes that courts have declined jurisdiction where the doctor advertised and listed his phone number in the forum state. (Prince, 49 Cal.App.4th at 1058.) Dr. Patterson's comments at the 2014 conference are analogous to what occurred in those cases. The other contacts cited by Mr. Teed (e.g. prescribing medicine, appearing on Good Morning America, being listed in international publications) are also insufficient to create jurisdiction.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
